WWW.PLATFORMINNOVATIONKIT.COM TABLE OF CONTENTS 01 INTRO Successful platform businesses leverage data and a modern IT infrastructure 03 PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE FRAMEWORK Guiding Principles of building a modern IT infrastructure 05 BEST PRACTICE USE CASES Examples of best-in-class / modern IT Architectures for platforms Learn more about platforms & ecosystem in our blog at platforminnovationkit.com 02 GUIDING PRINCIPLES Guiding Principles of building a modern IT infrastructure 04 PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE CANVAS Intro and How-To of using the Platform Architecture Canvas 06 ASSESSMENT A checklist for your architecture Intro Successful platforms are based on data and a reliable and scalable IT infrastructure Digital Disruption is happing now in all sectors dominated by the new rules of the platform economy 5 Increased Pressure for incumbent companies Losing contact with consumers & market insights Shrinking margins for product & asset heavy companies Pressure to build connected services for integrated consumer experiences Consumer World before Platforms World with Platforms Consumer ProducersEnablersRoles in the Value Chain: Platforms Adapted from: Kawohl, Krechting, 2018, www.ecosystemizer.com And platforms not stopping to expand their dominance 6 Adapted from: Kawohl, Krechting, 2018, www.ecosystemizer.com Amazon 1995-2000 Amazon 2019 Amazon covers with it’s offers and devices (Alexa, etc.) as Orchestrator and Realizer wide areas of our life. And increases continuously it’s B2B exposure. Cash LogisticsWeb ServicesPillpack Vehicle Space Travel Alexa Te a c h Str e e t ConsumerConsumer ... becoming an orchestrator in all life areas backed by data recognizing our consumer needs better than anybody else 7 Adapted from: Kawohl, Krechting, 2018, www.ecosystemizer.com Amazon 2025 Life Manager using artificial intelligence Health and education orchestrator and realizer using its gigantic database Mobility orchestrator and realizer through broad collaborative networks B2B only with Amazon, Data Handling & Analysis as key production factors Strong brand that creates trust deep into the private sphere Consumer The success of those platforms are mainly driven by network effects creating hyperscaling monopolies 8 NETWORK EFFECTS OCCURS WHEN A PRODUCT OR A SERVICE BECOMES MORE VALUABLE TO IT’S USERS AS MORE PEOPLE USE IT DEFINITION If Your Startup Doesn’t Have Network Effects, You Need to Rethink Your Strategy. In the last 23 years, network effects have accounted for 70% of the value creation in tech PLATFORMConsumerProducer + + users attraction usersattraction ... But also by building up the right capabilities on multiple layers to deliver exeptional experiences to the users 9 THE PLATFORM VALUE STACK Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem EngagementNetwork Data Things Sustainability Providing value by connecting supply & demand and fostering interaction and joint value creation Providing value by connecting data sources and provide insights to trigger positive behavior Providing value with physical & digital infrastructures to enable the layers above Providing value by generating positive impact to Society and our Planet (environment / nature / culture / social) For more information, please check our Platform Value Stack Advanced Tool & Insights Blog at platforminnovationkit.com ... To build those capabilities: first you must define the value proposition your platform wants to provide to the users 10 Value Layers Connects Demand & Supply Enables Transactions between User provide technical infrastructure for core service (e.g. marketplace website) platform provide complex workflow management capability Provides infrastructure for the market / ecosystem (e.g. OS) fosters co-creation Reduce Waste Creates bundled / integrated Experience Empowers Circular Economy Net positive / regenerative impact to ecosystem / society Generates insights to inform or drive actions Enables data logistics and exchange Self-learning to improve user experience Value provided by the Platformbasic superior Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Integration of different Data sources ... And secondly to define what IT capabilities are required to generate / deliver the value propositions 11 Value Layers Connects Demand & Supply provide technical infrastructure for core service (e.g. marketplace website) Reduce Waste Value Proposition To the users Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Integration of different Data sources Value Proposition To the users Automated Matchmaking User data validation Illu s tra tiv e Artificial Intelligence Data Interfaces Sensors Operating System Web Portal App Based on our analysis, most platforms lack key IT capabilities which slows down market traction and scalability 12 88% LIMITED INTEROPERABILITY Between ecosystem players leads to inefficient processes which lowers the core value proposition a platforms offers to the users 85% INABILITY TO DRAW MEANINGFUL INSIGHTS OUT OF DATA Mainly driven by the inability to collect the data and low capabilities to analyze insights and artificial intelligence to power through major data sets INEFFICIENT INTERACTIONS INABILITY TO SCALE 75% 73% SOURCE: Survey of +150 platform executives, fastbreak.one, 2021 A low velocity and liquidity of interaction between the platform stakeholders limits the possibility to monetize the platforms Especially driven by a highly complex IT infrastructure based on different systems and components Werner Vogels, CTO Amazon.com “You have to understand that the system you’re building now is not the system you will be running in 6 months or 2 years.” BEST PRACTICES A closer look at successful platform companies To understand the architecture of modern platforms we provide a closer look at three different types of platforms 15 PLATFORM TYPE B2B IoT Platform DESCRIPTION • Mindsphere is an Industrial IoT platform to connect machines, devices and provides services / insights based on the collected data • It‘s based on open standards to allow maximum interconnectivity PLATFORM TYPE C2C Marketplace DESCRIPTION Founded in 2008, San Francisco-based Airbnb is a community marketplace with over 7 million accommodations and more than 40,000 unique Experiences for customers to book on the company’s website or through its iOS and Android applications. PLATFORM TYPE B2C Sharing Platform DESCRIPTION • Lyft was founded in 2012 and is one of the largest transportation networks in the United States and Canada • Brings together rideshare, bikes, scooters, car rentals and transit all in one platform 16 PLATFORM TYPE C2C Marketplace PLATFORM VALUE STACK MATURITY 17 CORE VALUE PROPOSITION § is a community marketplace with over 7 million accommodations and more than 40,000 unique Experiences for customers to book on the company’s website or through its iOS and Android applications. INDUSTRY Accommodation / Travel Producers Partners Owner Platform Consumers Travelers Service Providers (Photographers, Cleaning Services, etc.) Hosts AirBnB The AirBnB Ecosystem The main challenge for AirBnB was it‘s significant growth in the first years after launch. With more and more users booking rooms from all over the world, the platform architecture needs to allow fast response times and fast data processing incl. Data exchance with other systems via API (e.g. payment providers). Additionally the systems needs to allow easy and reliable deployment of changes with zero downtime, as every minute down means multiple millions less revenue. https://developer.mindsphere.io/concepts/concept-architecture.html Ecosystem Engagement Intelligence Infrastructure 22 SOURCE: Siemens The major architecture components of MindSphere – The cloud-based, open IoT operating system from Siemens MindApps § Use apps from Siemens, partners or develop own apps § Gain asset transparency & analytical insights § Subscription based pricing model MindSphere § Open interface for development of customer specific apps § Various cloud infrastructures: SAP, AtoS, Microsoft Azure offered as public, private or on-premise MindConnect § Open standards for connectivity, e.g. OPC UA § Plug and play connection of Siemens and 3rd party products § Secure and ecrypted data communication 23 SOURCE: Siemens The applications from the partner ecosystem enable the users to create meaningful insights from the sensor data 24 SOURCE: Siemens IIoT Platforms are highly complex data platforms and require an excellent interplay between real world and virtual world 25 PLATFORM TYPE Sharing Platform 26 CORE VALUE PROPOSITION § Lyft is the fastest growing rideshare company in the United States and is available in more than 200 cities, facilitating 14 million rides per month. INDUSTRY B2C Sharing PLATFORM VALUE STACK MATURITY Producers Partners Owner Platform Consumers Travelers Service Partners (e.g. payment providers) Riders (provide service to transport the traveler) lyft The Dropbox Ecosystem Also for lyft the main challenges where scalability because of hyperscaling growth, reliability to ensure zero downtime because millions of travelers and riders rely on lyft. And data intelligence, because the GPS data of each rider is tracked and analyzed in real time to provide users the best riding options instantly. Therefore lyft is a great example how a platform provides immense value based on the data collected and at the same time to provide an easy to use experience. Ecosystem Engagement Intelligence Infrastructure 27 Illustration: of the Lyft Architecture and the services provided by the platform Web UI User Interfaces Platform Services Data Handling Core Platform AWS Services used Streaming Data analysis Data aggregation Storage Batch processing Route and Time calculation Adaptive pricing Cluster management Storage Scheduling Instance management Near-real time Analytics Mobile device CI/ CD Amazon Kubernetes Service Amazon DynamoDB Amazon RDS Amazon S3 AWS BatchAmazon EC2Amazon Kinesis 28 SUMMARY Different types of platforms require a different setup of the architecture Different types of platforms require different technology stack setups to deliver the right value to the users 29 PLATFORM TYPE B2B IoT Platform Platform Value Stack Maturity PLATFORM TYPE C2C Marketplace Platform Value Stack Maturity PLATFORM TYPE B2C Sharing Platform Platform Value Stack Maturity Ecosystem Engagement Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Layer Engagement Layer Intelligence Layer Infrastructure Layer To understand the architecture of modern platforms we provide a closer look at three different types of platforms 30 Value Stack Layer LyftMindsphereAirBnB As you can see, different types of platforms deliver their value propositions with strong focus on different layers. E.g. a C2C marketplace like AirBnB is strong on the engagement layer to enable direct communication and exchange between the users. Compared to a B2B platform like mindsphere, where the engagement / exchange between different companies in the value chain is still limited. But the core strength of an IoT platform is based on strong intelligence capabilities to provide e.g. data insights like predictive maintenance and to connect thousands of IoT devices. B2B C2C Value Maturity LevelMinimal Average Superior AWS WELL-ARCHITECTURED GUIDING PRINCIPLES 32 The shown case studies are build on AWS cloud infrastructure and underline the best practices AWS identified on the collected learnings from >1.000 platform projects around the globe. 33 AWS developed Strategies & best practices for architecting in the cloud aligned with 5 key pillars Security Reliability Performance efficiency Cost optimizationOperational excellence AWS Well-Architected Pillars Creating a software system is a lot like constructing a building. If the foundation is not solid, structural problems can undermine the integrity and function of the building. When architecting technology solutions, if you neglect the five pillars of operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, and cost optimization, it can become challenging to build a system that delivers on your expectations and requirements. Incorporating these pillars into your architecture helps produce stable and efficient systems. This allows you to focus on the other aspects of design, such as functional requirements. The AWS Well-Architected Framework helps cloud architects build the most secure, high-performing, resilient, and efficient infrastructure possible for their applications. 34 Key questions to answer when you design your Platform IT Architecture Security Reliability Performance efficiency Cost optimization Operational excellence § How do you plan to manage operational changes to the Platform? § How do you anticipate operational failures and plan accordingly? § How do you design for security across all layers? § How do you protect data in transit and at rest? § How do you scale horizontally to improve system availability? § How do you size for right capacity needs? § How do you plan to identify right size workloads? § How do you design to scale for multi geo, global platform users? § How do you intent to chose cost effective technology solutions? § How do you plan to measure the technology cost consumption? 35 OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE The Operational Excellence pillar includes the ability to support development and run workloads effectively, gain insight into their operation, and continuously improve supporting processes and procedures to delivery business value. Operational Excellence Whitepaper DESIGN PRINCIPLES Operations teams need to understand their business and customer needs so they can support business outcomes. Ops creates and uses procedures to respond to operational events, and validates their effectiveness to support business needs. Ops also collects metrics that are used to measure the achievement of desired business outcomes. Everything continues to change—your business context, business priorities, customer needs, etc. It’s important to design operations to support evolution over time in response to change and to incorporate lessons learned through their performance. Perform operations as code Make frequent, small, reversible changes Refine operations procedures frequently Anticipate failure Learn from all operational failures 36 SECURITY The Security pillar includes the ability to protect data, systems, and assets to take advantage of cloud technologies to improve your security. Security Whitepaper DESIGN PRINCIPLES Before you architect any workload, you need to put in place practices that influence security. You’ll want to control who can do what. In addition, you want to be able to identify security incidents, protect your systems and services, and maintain the confidentiality and integrity of data through data protection. You should have a well-defined and practiced process for responding to security incidents. These tools and techniques are important because they support objectives such as preventing financial loss or complying with regulatory obligations. Implement a strong identity foundation Enable traceability Apply security at all layers Automate security best practices Protect data in transit and at rest Keep people away from data Prepare for security events 37 RELIABILITY The Reliability pillar encompasses the ability of a workload to perform its intended function correctly and consistently when it’s expected to. This includes the ability to operate and test the workload through its total lifecycle. Reliability Whitepaper DESIGN PRINCIPLES To achieve reliability, you must start with the foundations—an environment where service quotas and network topology accommodate the workload. The workload architecture of the distributed system must be designed to prevent and mitigate failures. The workload must handle changes in demand or requirements, and it must be designed to detect failure and automatically heal itself. Before architecting any system, foundational requirements that influence reliability should be in place. For example, you must have sufficient network bandwidth to your data center. These requirements are sometimes neglected (because they are beyond a single project’s scope). This neglect can have a significant impact on the ability to deliver a reliable system. In an on-premises environment, these requirements can cause long lead times due to dependencies and therefore must be incorporated during initial planning. Automatically recover from failure Test recovery procedures Scale horizontally to increase aggregate workload availability Stop guessing capacity Manage change in automation 38 PERFORMANCE EFFICIENCY The Performance Efficiency pillar includes the ability to use computing resources efficiently to meet system requirements, and to maintain that efficiency as demand changes and technologies evolve. Performance Efficiency Whitepaper DESIGN PRINCIPLES Take a data-driven approach to building a high-performance architecture. Gather data on all aspects of the architecture, from the high-level design to the selection and configuration of resource types. Reviewing your choices on a regular basis ensures you are taking advantage of the continually evolving Cloud. Monitoring ensures you are aware of any deviance from expected performance. Make trade-offs in your architecture to improve performance, such as using compression or caching, or relaxing consistency requirements The optimal solution for a particular workload varies, and solutions often combine multiple approaches. Well- Architected workloads use multiple solutions and enable different features to improve performance Democratize advanced technologies Go global in minutes Use serverless architectures Experiment more often Consider mechanical sympathy 39 COST OPTIMIZATION The Cost Optimization pillar includes the ability to run systems to deliver business value at the lowest price point. Cost Optimization Whitepaper DESIGN PRINCIPLES As with the other pillars, there are trade-offs to consider. For example, do you want to optimize for speed to market or for cost? In some cases, it’s best to optimize for speed—going to market quickly, shipping new features, or simply meeting a deadline—rather than investing in up-front cost optimization. Design decisions are sometimes directed by haste rather than data, and as the temptation always exists to overcompensate rather than spend time benchmarking for the most cost-optimal deployment. This might lead to over-provisioned and under-optimized deployments. Using the appropriate services, resources, and configurations for your workloads is key to cost savings Implement cloud financial management Measure overall efficiency Adopt a consumption model Stop spending money on undifferentiated heavy lifting Analyze and attribute expenditure INTRODUCTION PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE FRAMEWORK 41 The Platform Architecture Framework provides guidance to select and build the right IT infrastructure PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE FRAMEWORK PLATFORM VALUE STACK AWS WELL-ARCHITECTURED FRAMEWORKOperational ExcellenceSecurityReliabilityPerformanceCost Optimization The Five Pillars The AWS Well-Architected Framework describes the key principles for designing and running workloads in the cloud and provides guidance for making improvements. Best of both worlds – The Platform Architecture Framework combines the Layers of the Platform Value Stack with the Well-Architectured Framework to give you guidance The Platform Value Stack provides guidance for platforms to enhance capabilities to provide exceptional value for the users of the platform and the surrounding ecosystem. Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Value Layers 42 The framework helps you to design the right IT architecture for your platform Operational Excellence Security Reliability Performance Cost Optimization The Five Pillars Intelligence Infrastructure Ecosystem Engagement Core and Sub Value Layers Marketing & Communication Personalization Connectivity Data Management, Machine Learning bus interfaces, processes events and messaging • Personalizes experience based on persona interest • Improve customer engagement through continued and sustained customer engagement • Provides server, for your platform IT architecture based on your business situation 45 To Process consists of 2 major steps: Designing and Improving your Architecture supported by our tools Design your Platform Business Model Design & Update your IT Architecture Check against Principles and improve Implement & Learn 1 2 The Platform Architecture Canvas supports you to identify and define the required IT components based on the 4 layers of the value stack. Additionally is supports you to identify key success factors. Define and Validate your Platform Business Model. Learn the key value propositions your platform provides to the users and clarify the required services. Tool Recommendations: § Platform Business Model Canvas § Platform Value Canvas § Platform Service Canvas The Well-Architectured Principles helps you to check your defined architecture against best practices and to identify areas for improvement or system critical components. Implement or Improve your IT infrastructure. Run validation experiments and gather feedback from users. Tool Recommendations: § Minimal Viable Platform Canvas What is the core components of my platform IT architecture? The Platform Service Canvas helps you to understand and define required services to onboard user, perform the matchmaking, conduct transaction and to keep them engaged on the platform. 1. Define the key users of your platform 2. List the major steps of the user journey (e.g. onboarding, matchmaking, transaction, ...) 3. List the critical components on each layer of the stack 1. Engagement = Touchpoints with the users 2. Integration = Interfaces to your core platform 3. Intelligence = data driven services 4. Infrastructure = operational / fundamental systemsPLATFORM ARCHITECTURE CANVAS HOW TO PURPOSE 1 2 3 46PLATFORM SERVICE CANVAS AirBnB Traveller Host Cleaning Service Onboarding Matchmaking Transaction Engagement Onboarding Matchmaking Transaction Engagement Matchmaking Transaction Digital Keys Event Streaming Change Data capture Bulk Data import Block Store EMR Batch processing Listing service Pricing Service Cluster management Storage Workflow Scheduling Search Service Mobile App Instance management Web UI Booking Service Payment Service SmartphoneNotebook / PC APIs to reservation systems Network 47 ASSESSMENT How well architectured is your platform? The Platform Architecture Assessment helps you to assess how good you implemented the Well Architecture Principles. Based on your assessment you can identify areas for improvement and prioritize activities together with your leadership & tech team. 1. Score each individual criteria together with your team – consider the sub-criteria 2. Discuss the results with your team 3. Give your architecture an overall score with the status date of the assessment. This helps you to monitor progress and trends. 4. Present the results to your leadership team with clear priorities and actions for further improvements - always linked to the overall platform strategy and business modelPLATFORM ARCHITECTURE ASSESSMENT HOW TO PURPOSE 49 LET’S BUILD A BETTER TOMORROW
